title: Vegan cashew cheesecake
subtitle: No-bake, no-dairy cheesecake with a date and nut base
excerpt: A no-bake vegan cheesecake built on soaked cashews blended to a thick, creamy filling. The base is dates and desiccated coconut. It sets in the freezer and is sliced from frozen.
sourceNotes: Cashew-based vegan cheesecakes developed within raw food and vegan communities in the 2000s and were popularised through food blogs and social media from around 2012. The technique of soaking cashews overnight and blending them to a smooth cream is a workaround for the missing dairy fat and protein structure. This recipe draws on methods developed by Ella Mills in Deliciously Ella (2015) and Sarah Britton in My New Roots (2015).

recipe.freezeNotes: The cheesecake is served from frozen. Keep in the freezer for up to 1 month. Slice directly from frozen; it will soften to a perfect consistency within 5 minutes at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cheesecake must be made at least 8 hours ahead (overnight is ideal). It keeps in the freezer for 1 month.

baking.decoratingTechnique: fresh berries and mint scattered over the top just before serving

content.text: The cashews must soak for at least 6 hours (overnight is better) to soften enough to blend completely smooth. Unsoaked or partly soaked cashews leave a grainy texture that no amount of blending will remove. The soaking water is discarded; use fresh water for blending.

A high-powered blender gives the smoothest result. A food processor works but leaves a slightly coarser texture. Blend for at least 3 minutes, scraping down the sides frequently. The filling should be completely smooth with no visible cashew pieces before it goes into the tin.

attrs.prepNote: raw, soaked in cold water for 6 hours, drained

content.text: Line a 20 cm springform tin with baking paper. Process the stoned {{medjool-dates}}, {{desiccated-coconut}}, {{walnuts}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} in a food processor until the mixture comes together as a sticky mass. Press firmly and evenly into the base of the tin. Freeze for 30 minutes while you make the filling.
Blend the filling and set, 8 hours

Drain the soaked {{cashews}} and rinse well. Blend with the {{coconut-oil}}, {{maple-syrup}}, {{lemon-juice}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} until completely smooth, at least 3 minutes. Pour over the frozen base and smooth the top. Freeze for at least 8 hours or overnight.

Remove from the freezer 5 minutes before serving. Release the springform and arrange the {{blueberries}} and sliced {{strawberries}} over the top. Slice with a sharp knife dipped in hot water and dried between cuts. Return any leftover cheesecake to the freezer.
